steps.title: Identify the cost structure
steps.content: The bus costs a flat \$950 for the first 3 hours. For each hour beyond 3 hours, there is an additional \$50 charge. With $$t$$ total hours, the extra hours are $$t - 3$$.

steps.title: Write the equation
steps.content:
Total cost = flat fee + (extra hours) × (hourly rate):
$$950 + 50(t - 3) = 1{,}150$$

steps.title: Match to the answer choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '$950(t-3)+50t=1{,}150$': Multiplies the flat fee by the variable extra hours, which makes no sense structurally. ✗
  • Choice B — '$950(3t)+50t=1{,}150$': Also incorrectly multiplies the flat fee by time, and uses total hours for the variable rate. ✗
  • Choice C — '$950+50(t-3)=1{,}150$': Correctly adds the fixed \$950 to \$50 times the number of hours beyond 3. ✓
  • Choice D — '$950+50(3t)=1{,}150$': Uses $$3t$$ instead of $$(t-3)$$ for extra hours — that would triple the total time, not subtract the first 3 hours. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
